title: Revision Selection, Ranges, Search, and Change Discovery · Repository Checkpoint
summary: Answer a focused history question without checking out candidate commits or mistaking range notation for set subtraction in every context. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a revision-query and change-discovery workbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Resolve full and abbreviated object IDs, refs, reflog selectors, ancestry suffixes, and peel operators with ambiguity checks.
Interpret two-dot, three-dot, negative revisions, symmetric difference, and command-specific diff behavior.
Use pathspec magic, exclusions, attributes, top-level anchoring, and separator placement without confusing paths with revisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Feature tip F has 5 commits after merge base M while main has two. Compare M..F, main...F, HEAD~2, and path-limited pickaxe results without confusing commit sets with file diffs. Competency probe: Choose a review range that matches branch intent, merge base, stacked work, and force-push risk.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose a review range that matches branch intent, merge base, stacked work, and force-push risk.

content.text: Feature tip F has 5 commits after merge base M while main has two. Compare M..F, main...F, HEAD~2, and path-limited pickaxe results without confusing commit sets with file diffs. Competency probe: Choose a review range that matches branch intent, merge base, stacked work, and force-push risk.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Change Review Ranges symptom in a revision-query and change-discovery workbook during this quiz case.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Pro Git, Second Edition — Controls cumulative conceptual sequence from setup through internals, collaboration, tooling, and distributed workflows.

hints: Name the Change Review Ranges precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Feature tip F has 5 commits after merge base M while main has two. Compare M..F, main...F, HEAD~2, and path-limited pickaxe results without confusing commit sets with file diffs. Competency probe: Choose a review range that matches branch intent, merge base, stacked work, and force-push risk.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Comparing two branch tips always isolates only the proposed work.

stimulus.title: Change Review Ranges failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Comparing two branch tips always isolates only the proposed work.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose a review range that matches branch intent, merge base, stacked work, and force-push risk.

options.text: Run the Change Review Ranges boundary case for a revision-query and change-discovery workbook, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the Change Review Ranges result because visible a revision-query and change-discovery workbook output is the highest authority. This does not resolve diagnose the hidden failure.

For diagnose the hidden failure, apply Change Review Ranges without its stated preconditions, omit the a revision-query and change-discovery workbook trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Change Review Ranges for a revision-query and change-discovery workbook.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Change Review Ranges: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Feature tip F has 2 commits after merge base M while main has two. Compare M..F, main...F, HEAD~2, and path-limited pickaxe results without confusing commit sets with file diffs. Competency probe: Choose a review range that matches branch intent, merge base, stacked work, and force-push risk.
steps.why: Retrieving Change Review Ranges through the concrete a revision-query and change-discovery workbook case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose a review range that matches branch intent, merge base, stacked work, and force-push risk.
